ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "compound" REAL /* Compound sentiment score of the tweet */,
  "negative" REAL /* Negative sentiment score of the tweet */,
  "neutral" REAL /* Neutral sentiment score of the tweet */,
  "positive" REAL /* Positive sentiment score of the tweet */,
  "tweet_count" INTEGER /* Count of tweets by the user */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who made the tweet */,
  "tweet_text" TEXT /* Text of the tweet */,
  "tweet_date" TEXT /* Date the tweet was posted */,
  "tweet_url" TEXT /* URL of the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user on social media */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "joined_date" TEXT /* Date the user joined the social media platform */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile picture */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiment_analysis_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "topic" TEXT /* Topic the report pertains to */,
  "average_compound" REAL /* Average compound sentiment score for the topic */,
  "total_tweets" INTEGER /* Total number of tweets analyzed in the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "generated_by" TEXT /* Name or ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_file" TEXT /* File path or URL of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiment_analysis_reports_topic FOREIGN KEY ("topic") REFERENCES topics ("topic_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "topics" (
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each topic */,
  "topic_name" TEXT /* Name of the topic */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the topic */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the topic */,
  "sub_category" TEXT /* Sub-category of the topic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user engaging with the content */,
  "engagement_date" TEXT /* Date of the engagement */,
  "views" INTEGER /* Number of views for the engagement */,
  "shares" INTEGER /* Number of shares for the engagement */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes for the engagement */,
  "engagement_type" TEXT /* Type of engagement (e.g., view, share, like, comment) */,
  "content_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content being engaged with */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_engagement_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user subscribed to notifications */,
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the topic for which notifications are sent */,
  "is_subscribed" INTEGER /* Whether the user is subscribed to notifications or not */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the notification record was created */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, push, in-app) */,
  "notification_status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., sent, read, unread) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_topic_id FOREIGN KEY ("topic_id") REFERENCES topics ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hashtags" (
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hashtag record */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet associated with the hashtag */,
  "hashtag_text" TEXT /* Text of the hashtag */,
  "hashtag_category" TEXT /* Category of the hashtag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hashtag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hashtags_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)
